HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

Modules » Simulation domains

 Static Public Member Functions inherited from Domain
static void EnableAccurate ()
 Enable globally more accurate (and usually slower) evaluation.
 
 Static Protected Attributes inherited from Domain
static bool NeedsAccurate = false
 Flag for accurate computation.
 

Detailed Description

3D stratified (layered) medium domain.

Responsibilities on top of Domain:

  • Keep per‑layer material parameters and interface z‑positions.
  • Build per‑layer homogeneous Green's functions plus a layered Green's function with Sommerfeld integration (tabulated on problem‑dependent grids).
  • Partition arbitrary point sets into layer‑consistent groups (slice).
  • Compute tabulation ranges and grids (calculateRange, computeGrid).
  • Provide layered incident sources (PlaneWave, Dipole, Gaussian).
  • Post‑processing: scattered/total fields and cross sections with layered Green's function.

Definition at line 38 of file DomainLayered3D.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ DomainLayered3D() [1/2]

DomainLayered3D::DomainLayered3D ( SurfaceMesh * inMesh,
int inDIndex,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inEpsilon,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inMu,
const std::vector< double > & inZVals,
dcmplx inVacuumWavelength )

Constructs a 3D layered domain with given material properties and geometry.

This constructor initializes the domain with the provided surface mesh, domain index, permittivity, permeability, and interface positions. It also calculates the layer thicknesses, reads positional data, slices points, computes the tabulation grids, and initializes the Green's function.

Parameters
inMeshPointer to the surface mesh.
inDIndexDomain index.
inEpsilonVector of relative permittivity values for each layer.
inMuVector of relative permeability values for each layer.
inZValsVector of Z-coordinates defining the layer interfaces.
inVacuumWavelengthThe wavelength in vacuum.

DomainLayered3D::DomainLayered3D ( SurfaceMesh * inMesh,
int inDIndex,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inEpsilon,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inMu,
const std::vector< double > & inZVals,
dcmplx inVacuumWavelength,
bool parent )

Construct without creating Green's functions (owner/aggregate path).

Parameters
inMeshPointer to the surface mesh.
inDIndexDomain index.
inEpsilonVector of relative permittivity values for each layer.
inMuVector of relative permeability values for each layer.
inZValsVector of Z-coordinates defining the layer interfaces.
inVacuumWavelengthThe wavelength in vacuum.
parentIf true, skip InitGrnFun(); caller is responsible to call it later.

◆ ~DomainLayered3D()

DomainLayered3D::~DomainLayered3D ( )

Destructor: deletes layered/homogeneous GreenF objects and utilities.

Note
grnFun is an array allocated with new[] in InitGrnFun(), so delete[] is used here.

rvec propagationDirection,
cvec polarization )
virtual

Add a PlaneWave excitation adapted to the layered medium.

Parameters
wavelengthVacuum wavelength \(\lambda_0\) (complex allowed).
propagationDirectionUnit vector \(\hat{\mathbf k}\) (direction of travel).

0 on success; non-zero otherwise.

Definition at line 353 of file DomainLayered3D.cpp.

◆ calculateRange()

Range DomainLayered3D::calculateRange ( const PositionGroup & pts,
double rmin = 0.01,
double zmin = 0.01,
double margin = 0.05 )

Calculates the tabulation range for a group of points.

Parameters
ptsGroup of positions.
rminMinimum radial range.
zminMinimum vertical range.
marginAdditional margin buffer.
Returns
Computed range object.

This function determines the spatial range in both the radial and vertical (Z) directions for a given set of points in a layered medium. It ensures that the computed range remains within valid boundaries while applying necessary adjustments using a margin parameter.

Definition at line 497 of file DomainLayered3D.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ ClearIncidentFields()

int DomainLayered3D::ClearIncidentFields ( )
overridevirtual

Clears all incident fields and resets the cached layered Green's function for field evaluation.

This method first calls the base class implementation to clear the \( \text{incidentFields} \) list (deleting the Dipole objects). It then deletes and nullifies the \( \text{grnFunLayeredFields} \) pointer. This is crucial because Dipole objects are recreated for each subjob, but the Domain object persists. Resetting \( \text{grnFunLayeredFields} \) forces \( \text{SimJob} \) to call \( \text{newGrnFunLayered()} \) again, which correctly wires the new Dipole sources.

Reimplemented from Domain.

Definition at line 1254 of file DomainLayered3D.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ computeGrid()

std::vector< Grid > DomainLayered3D::computeGrid ( const std::vector< PositionGroup > & pos,
int nr = 20,
int nz = 20,
double rmin = 1e-2,
double margin = 0.05,
double zmin = 1e-2 )

Computes the grid points for tabulation.

Parameters
posVector of grouped positions.
nrNumber of radial grid points.
nzNumber of vertical grid points.
rminMinimum radial range.
marginAdditional margin buffer.
zminMinimum vertical range.
Returns
Computed grid points.

This function generates a structured grid in the radial and vertical (Z) directions for each position group, ensuring appropriate spacing and range adjustments. It uses the calculated spatial bounds to define the grid points.

◆ newGrnFunLayered()

void DomainLayered3D::newGrnFunLayered ( std::vector< rvec > & posvec)
virtual

Initialize the layered Green's function used for field evaluation.

Parameters
posvecCloud of observation points; only those inside the domain are used.

Builds a new set of tabulation grids adapted to posvec, creates a dedicated layered Green's function for field evaluation, and wires any dipole incidents to their own post‑processing Green's function.

Implements Domain.

Definition at line 299 of file DomainLayered3D.cpp.

◆ slice()

std::vector< PositionGroup > DomainLayered3D::slice ( const std::vector< rvec > & pos1,
std::vector< rvec > * pos2_opt = nullptr )

Computes the slicing of points with respect to interfaces.

Parameters
pos1Primary set of positions.
pos2_optOptional secondary set of positions.
Returns
Vector of grouped positions.

With two sets (pos1, pos2), all unique layer‑pair combinations are produced. If pos2 has a single point, three ghost points are added (x,y,z offsets) to stabilize range estimation near singular pairs.

◆ writeFields()

int DomainLayered3D::writeFields ( dcmplx krho,
double start,
double end,
int numPoints,
double sourcePointZ,
const std::string & waves )

Compute fields over a 1D sweep and write them to a text file.

Parameters
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
startStart value of the sweep parameter.
endEnd value of the sweep parameter.
numPointsNumber of uniformly spaced points in the sweep.
sourcePointZSource point z-coordinate.
wavesWave type selector ("TE" or "TM").
Returns
0 on success; non-zero on I/O or computation error.
